heading: AUKUS partnership oversight and accountability framework
label: 10411 AUKUS partnership oversight and accountability framework

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of State shall designate a senior advisor at the Department of State (in this section referred to as the “Senior Advisor”), who shall oversee and coordinate the implementation of the AUKUS partnership.

children.heading: Qualification
children.label: B Qualification
children.text: The Senior Advisor may be an individual serving within the existing leadership of the Department of State but that individual may not hold any other position concurrently while serving as the Senior Advisor.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: C Reporting
children.text: The Senior Advisor shall report directly to the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: D Guidance
children.text: The Secretary of State shall issue guidance to all bureaus of the Department of State specifying the Senior Advisor’s responsibility for coordinating the implementation of all AUKUS partnership-related activities.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate efforts to implement the AUKUS partnership across relevant bureaus, directorates, and offices of the Department of State involved in matters such as arms exports, non-proliferation, deterrence, security assistance, and Indo-Pacific and United Kingdom relations;

children.label: B
children.text: serve as the lead within the Department of State on matters relating to the AUKUS partnership in the interagency process;

children.label: C
children.text: lead diplomatic efforts related to the AUKUS partnership with other governments to explain how the partnership will enhance security and stability in the Indo-Pacific region; and

children.label: i
children.text: Australia’s acquisition of conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarines;

children.label: ii
children.text: jointly developing advanced military capabilities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any new programs under the AUKUS partnership.

children.heading: Personnel to support the Senior Advisor
children.label: 3 Personnel to support the Senior Advisor
children.text: The Secretary of State shall ensure that the Senior Advisor is adequately staffed with respect to the Senior Advisor’s duties described in paragraph (2) through details, or assignment of employees of the Department of State, with expertise consistent with such duties.

children.label: A
children.text: shall meet regularly to coordinate internally on issues relating to the implementation of the AUKUS partnership; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be led by the Senior Advisor.

children.label: A
children.text: ensuring that responsible offices maintain a unified list of all defense-related transactions that have taken place under the AUKUS partnership;

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring the establishment of a framework for gathering, maintaining, and exchanging information relating to companies, individuals, or entities that are compromising security of military technology, defense articles, and defense services exchanged under the AUKUS partnership; and

children.label: C
children.text: establishing an AUKUS industry forum for industry stakeholders, including non-traditional defense contractors (as such term is defined in ), that will be open for the participation of foreign industry involved in the AUKUS partnership.section 3014 of title 10

children.heading: Personnel to support the Task Force
children.label: 3 Personnel to support the Task Force
children.text: The personnel assigned to support the Senior Advisor under subsection (a)(3) shall also support the Task Force. The Secretary of State may not assign any additional personnel to support the Task Force.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: c Notification
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , or not later than 90 days after the date on which a senior advisor at the Department of State is designated as the Senior Advisor, whichever occurs earlier, the Secretary of State shall notify the appropriate congressional committees of the number of personnel, relevant expertise of such personnel, and duties of such personnel directly supporting the work of the Senior Advisor and the offices supporting the Task Force.December 22, 2023
A detailed description of the planned work of the Senior Advisor and the Task Force on matters related to the implementation of the AUKUS partnership.

children.label: A
children.text: the average and median times for the United States Government to review applications for licenses to export defense articles or defense services to persons, corporations, and the governments (including agencies and subdivisions of such governments, including official missions of such governments) of Australia or the United Kingdom;

children.label: B
children.text: the average and median times for the United States Government to review applications from Australia and the United Kingdom for foreign military sales beginning from the date Australia or the United Kingdom submitted a letter of request that resulted in a letter of acceptance; and

children.label: C
children.text: the number of applications from Australia and the United Kingdom for licenses to export defense articles and defense services that were denied or approved with provisos, listed by year.

children.label: A
children.text: any instance of unauthorized access to technical data or defense articles;

children.label: B
children.text: inadequate physical or cyber security;

children.label: C
children.text: retransfers or re-exports without authorization; and

children.label: D
children.text: employees of foreign companies that are United States persons that provide defense services without authorization.

a detailed description of any issues that representatives of the United States, the United Kingdom, or Australia have identified that threaten or conflict with the stated goals of the AUKUS partnership and any efforts to resolve these issues;
information on the National Disclosure Policy Committee with respect to adoption of a classification category relating to any anticipatory disclosure policy for Australia and the United Kingdom;

a detailed description of Department of State investigations into violations under or related provisions that involve AUKUS partners or entities in the United States, the United Kingdom, and Australia;section 2778 of this title
details on whether regulatory changes to exemptions authorized under subsection () of are likely or necessary within the next year; andlsection 2778 of this title

an assessment of the change in the average and median Department of State licensing review times for the current reporting year based on the average and median licensing review times from the prior calendar year, including review times across the interagency for export licenses issued to Australia or the United Kingdom.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the position of the Senior Advisor and the Task Force shall terminate on the date that is 7 years after .December 22, 2023

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: The Secretary of State may renew the position of the Senior Advisor and the Task Force for 1 additional period of 4 years beginning after the date on which the Secretary notifies the appropriate congressional committees of the renewal.
the Committee on Foreign Affairs and Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and
the Committee on Foreign Relations and Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.
